seoTitle: How to Withdraw Money From Gumroad to Your Bank
metaDescription: Understand how Gumroad's automatic payout system works, including the weekly schedule, minimum balance, and linking a bank account or PayPal.
intro: Gumroad pays creators automatically rather than requiring a manual withdrawal request, but you still need to set up your payout details correctly. This guide explains how the payout schedule works, what minimum balance is required, and how to connect a bank account or PayPal so your earnings land where you expect.

steps.heading: Open your Gumroad settings
steps.body: Head to your dashboard's settings area to find the section where payout preferences are managed.

steps.heading: Review the payout schedule and minimum balance
steps.body: Gumroad pays out automatically on a recurring basis once your balance clears a minimum threshold, so check what that threshold currently is.

steps.heading: Add your bank or PayPal details
steps.body: Enter your banking information or connect a verified PayPal account so Gumroad knows where to send your earnings.

steps.heading: Verify your identity
steps.body: Complete any required identity or tax verification steps, since payouts can be held until this is finished.

steps.heading: Monitor your balance
steps.body: Keep an eye on your available versus pending balance in the dashboard to track when your next payout is coming.

faq.question: How often does Gumroad pay out earnings?
faq.answer: Gumroad pays creators automatically on a regular recurring schedule once their balance clears the minimum threshold.

faq.question: What's the minimum balance to get paid?
faq.answer: Gumroad requires your balance to reach a set minimum before it's included in the next automatic payout.

faq.question: Can I get paid via PayPal instead of a bank account?
faq.answer: Yes, Gumroad lets you connect a verified PayPal account as an alternative to direct bank deposit.

faq.question: Why is my payout delayed?
faq.answer: Delays usually happen due to incomplete identity verification, missing tax documentation, or balances below the payout threshold.
